1	---
2	ddx:
3	  id: FEAT-005
4	  depends_on:
5	    - helix.prd
6	    - FEAT-007
7	---
8	# Feature: Artifact Convention
9	
10	**ID:** FEAT-005
11	**Status:** In Progress
12	**Priority:** P1
13	**Owner:** DDx Team
14	
15	## Overview
16	
17	An **artifact** is any file carrying DDx **identity** — a structured `id` plus
18	optional graph and provenance metadata. Identity may be embedded (in a
19	markdown file's YAML frontmatter under a `ddx:` block) or supplied via a
20	sidecar `<filename>.ddx.yaml` for non-markdown media (diagrams, wireframes,
21	images, prompts, audio, PDFs, binaries, etc.).
22	
23	The `id` field identifies the artifact; the ID prefix conventionally
24	indicates the type (ADR-001, SD-003, MET-007, FEAT-012, etc.). DDx does
25	not hardcode artifact types — it manages the document graph generically.
26	Types are conventions that workflows define.
27	
28	This replaces the previous approach of dedicated `ddx adr` and `ddx sd` commands. Those are removed in favor of the generic `ddx doc` commands (FEAT-007).
29	
30	## Definition
31	
32	### Authority rule
33	
34	**Identity present → artifact.** A file is an artifact if and only if DDx can
35	resolve identity for it, by either of two mechanisms:
36	
37	1. **Embedded identity** — markdown files with a `ddx:` block in YAML
38	   frontmatter:
39	
40	   ```yaml
41	   ---
42	   ddx:
43	     id: <PREFIX>-<NNN>
44	     depends_on: [<other-ids>]
45	   ---
46	   # Content
47	   ```
48	
49	2. **Sidecar identity** — any file `<path>/<name>.<ext>` accompanied by
50	   `<path>/<name>.<ext>.ddx.yaml` containing the identity block:
51	
52	   ```yaml
53	   # diagram.excalidraw.ddx.yaml
54	   id: DIAG-007
55	   media_type: application/vnd.excalidraw+json
56	   depends_on: [SD-003]
57	   generated_by:
58	     run: <run-id>
59	     prompt: <input-selector>
60	     source_hash: <sha256>
61	   ```
62	
63	There is no second-class "managed asset" concept. Either a file has identity
64	(and is an artifact), or it does not (and DDx ignores it for graph purposes).
65	
66	DDx uses content-based discovery: it scans for embedded `ddx:` frontmatter
67	in markdown and for sidecar `*.ddx.yaml` files within the project's
68	configured roots, subject to an exclusion list of tool-managed and storage
69	directories (e.g. `.git/`, `.ddx/`, `.ddx/plugins/`, `.claude/`, `.agents/`,
70	and any `worktrees/` subtree). Identity comes from frontmatter, not from
71	file paths, but discovery is bounded so plugin-shipped or agent-scratch
72	copies of canonical docs do not collide with the project's own artifacts.
73	
74	### Common Artifact Types (by convention)
75	
76	| Prefix | Type | Typical Location |
77	|--------|------|-----------------|
78	| `FEAT` | Feature specification | `docs/helix/01-frame/features/` |
79	| `ADR` | Architecture Decision Record | `docs/adr/` or `docs/helix/02-design/adr/` |
80	| `SD` | Solution Design | `docs/designs/` or `docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/` |
81	| `TD` | Technical Design | `docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/` |
82	| `TP` | Test Plan | `docs/helix/03-test/` |
83	| `MET` | Metric definition | `docs/metrics/` |
84	| `US` | User Story | `docs/helix/01-frame/user-stories/` |
85	| `DIAG` | Diagram (mermaid, excalidraw, svg) | `docs/diagrams/` |
86	| `WIRE` | Wireframe / mockup | `docs/wireframes/` |
87	| `IMG` | Image asset (hero, illustration) | `docs/images/` |
88	| `PRMT` | Prompt artifact | `docs/prompts/` |
89	| `RSCH` | Research note (IDs `RSCH-NNN`) | `docs/helix/00-discover/research/` |
90	| `REF` | Reference, external source capture (IDs `REF-NNN`) | `docs/helix/00-discover/references/` |
91	
92	These prefixes are conventions — DDx treats them all the same. Workflows
93	(HELIX) may enforce that certain types exist or follow specific templates.
94	
95	Projects may introduce additional conventions such as `AC-*` for acceptance
96	criteria or `TC-*` for test cases. DDx does not privilege those types; it only
97	tracks their IDs and relationships.
98	
99	### Identity Schema
100	
101	Identity carries the same field set whether embedded under a markdown
102	`ddx:` block or stored in a sidecar `.ddx.yaml`.
103	
104	| Field | Type | Required | Description |
105	|-------|------|----------|-------------|
106	| `id` | string | yes | Unique identifier with type prefix (e.g., `ADR-001`) |
107	| `media_type` | string | conditional | IANA-style media type. Required for sidecar identity; inferred from extension for embedded markdown (`text/markdown`). |
108	| `depends_on` | []string | no | IDs of artifacts this one depends on |
109	| `inputs` | []string | no | Input selectors for prompt context resolution |
110	| `generated_by` | object | no | Provenance for generated artifacts: `{ run, prompt, source_hash }`. Records the layer-1/layer-2 run that produced this artifact and the hash of its source inputs. |
111	| `review` | object | no | Staleness tracking (managed by `ddx doc stamp`) |
112	| `prompt` | string | no | Custom prompt for updating this document |
113	| `parking_lot` | bool | no | Skip in staleness checks |
114	
115	Unknown fields are preserved on round-trip.
116	
117	#### Type-specific metadata (outside `ddx:`)
118	
119	Type conventions may carry additional metadata fields at the top level of
120	frontmatter, alongside (not inside) the `ddx:` identity block. The parser
121	treats these as inert pass-through fields. For example, a `REF-NNN`
122	reference artifact captures provenance about an external source:
123	
124	```yaml
125	---
126	ddx:
127	  id: REF-007
128	  depends_on: [RSCH-003]
129	source_url: https://example.com/article
130	source_author: Jane Doe
131	accessed: 2026-05-01
132	---
133	# Reference: ...
134	```
135	
136	Identity (`id`, `depends_on`, etc.) stays under `ddx:`; type-specific fields
137	like `source_url`, `source_author`, and `accessed` live at the top level.
138	
139	#### `media_type`
140	
141	Identifies how an artifact's bytes should be interpreted. Examples:
142	
143	- `text/markdown` (default for embedded identity)
144	- `image/png`, `image/svg+xml`
145	- `application/vnd.excalidraw+json`
146	- `application/pdf`
147	- `text/mermaid`
148	- `application/vnd.ddx.prompt+yaml`
149	
150	Web UI rendering, regenerate flows, and tooling key off `media_type` rather
151	than file extension.
152	
153	#### `generated_by`
154	
155	Marks an artifact as the output of a DDx run. Carries the run id, the prompt
156	selector, and the `source_hash` used at generation time. Regeneration is
157	source-hash-driven: when inputs change, the source hash diverges and tooling
158	can offer to regenerate. `generated_by` creates a separate provenance edge in
159	the graph (FEAT-007) — it tracks origin, not authority, and does **not**
160	cascade staleness like `depends_on`.
161	
162	## The Artifact Graph
163	
164	All artifacts in a project — markdown and non-markdown alike — form a
165	**single directed acyclic graph** via `depends_on` edges. This graph is the
166	project's authority structure: it encodes which artifacts govern which.
167	
168	```
169	Product Vision
170	  └─ PRD
171	       ├─ FEAT-001
172	       │    ├─ SD-001
173	       │    │    ├─ TD-001
174	       │    │    │    └─ TP-001
175	       │    │    └─ DIAG-001  (excalidraw, sidecar identity)
176	       │    └─ US-001
177	       ├─ FEAT-002
178	       │    └─ ADR-001
179	       └─ MET-001
180	```
181	
182	Every artifact should be reachable from a root (typically the vision or PRD). Orphaned artifacts — those with no incoming or outgoing edges — are a smell that `ddx doc validate` flags.
183	
184	### Graph Properties
185	
186	- **Single graph per project.** All artifacts with identity participate in one graph regardless of media type. There are no separate "ADR graph," "diagram graph," or "feature graph" partitions.
187	- **Edges are explicit.** Only `depends_on` creates authority edges. `generated_by` creates a parallel provenance edge that is not authority. No implicit edges from directory structure or naming.
188	- **Direction is authority.** A `depends_on` edge from B to A means "A governs B" — if A changes, B may be stale.
189	- **Authority staleness cascades.** If a node is stale, all its `depends_on` descendants are transitively stale.
190	- **Provenance staleness does not cascade.** A `generated_by` source-hash mismatch flags only the generated artifact itself, offering regeneration; it does not propagate.
191	- **Types and media are orthogonal.** The graph doesn't care about artifact types or media. An ADR can depend on a feature spec; a metric can depend on a PRD; a wireframe can depend on a solution design. The graph is type- and media-agnostic.
192	
193	## What DDx Does With Artifacts
194	
195	All via `ddx doc` commands (FEAT-007):
196	
197	1. **Discover** — scan for embedded `ddx:` frontmatter in markdown and for sidecar `*.ddx.yaml` files
198	2. **Graph** — build the single project-wide dependency DAG plus provenance edges
199	3. **Stale** — detect when upstream `depends_on` artifacts changed since last stamp; detect generated artifacts whose `source_hash` no longer matches
200	4. **Stamp** — record review hashes after updating a document
201	5. **List** — list artifacts with optional type or media filtering (`ddx doc list --type ADR`, `ddx artifact list --media-type image/png`)
202	6. **Show** — display artifact content/metadata; for non-markdown media, show the sidecar plus a media-typed pointer
203	7. **Validate** — check identity structure, dependency references exist, no circular `depends_on`, no orphans, sidecars match an existing target file
204	
205	## What DDx Does NOT Do
206	
207	- **Scaffold from templates** — agents and workflows create artifacts. DDx doesn't need `create` commands for each type.
208	- **Enforce type-specific structure** — "ADRs must have a Decision section" is a workflow concern. DDx validates the graph; dun validates content structure.
209	- **Hardcode artifact types or media** — no switch statements on type prefixes or media types. The graph treats all artifacts equally.
210	- **Partition the graph** — all artifacts are in one graph regardless of type, media, or directory.
211	- **Execute artifact files directly** — runtime execution belongs to DDx runs (FEAT-010), not the artifact graph itself.
212	- **Recognize a "managed asset" tier** — files without identity are not artifacts, period. There is no halfway state.
213	
214	## Templates and Prompts
215	
216	Each artifact type has associated resources in the document library:
217	
218	```
219	.ddx/library/artifacts/<type>/
220	├── template.md       # Structural template (frontmatter + sections), or sidecar template for binary types
221	├── create.md         # Prompt for creating a new instance
222	├── evolve.md         # Prompt for updating when dependencies change
223	└── check.md          # Prompt for reviewing/validating the artifact
224	```
225	
226	For example, `.ddx/library/artifacts/adr/`:
227	- `template.md` — ADR skeleton with Context/Decision/Alternatives sections
228	- `create.md` — prompt instructing an agent how to write a good ADR
229	- `evolve.md` — prompt for updating an ADR when upstream requirements change
230	- `check.md` — prompt for reviewing an ADR against its governing artifacts
231	
232	These are **library resources**, not CLI features. Agents and workflows read them from the library when operating on artifacts. DDx ships defaults; projects can override with project-specific versions.
233	
234	The doc graph (FEAT-007) can resolve these prompts via input selectors:
235	- `artifact-prompt:ADR:create` → the create prompt for ADR artifacts
236	- `artifact-template:ADR` → the template for ADR artifacts
237	
238	## Artifacts and Runs
239	
240	Artifacts stay declarative. They define meaning, authority, provenance, and
241	relationships in the graph. DDx does **not** execute artifact files directly.
242	
243	Runtime invocation belongs to the three-layer run architecture (FEAT-010):
244	`ddx run` (layer-1 single agent invocation), `ddx try <bead>` (layer-2 bead
245	attempt), `ddx work` (layer-3 queue drain). Run records are file-backed and
246	linked to artifacts by ID via `generated_by`.
247	
248	### Boundary
249	
250	- Artifacts declare what matters, who governs it, where it came from, and how
251	  it relates to other artifacts in the graph.
252	- Runs describe one invocation that may produce or consume artifacts.
253	- Run records capture one immutable invocation: raw logs, structured result
254	  data, status, tokens/duration/exit, and provenance.
255	- Runs and run records are not artifacts and do not participate in document
256	  staleness or graph validation.
257	- DDx does not infer run semantics from an artifact type prefix.
258	
259	### Examples
260	
261	- A `MET-*` artifact may link to a layer-1 run definition that emits numeric
262	  observations over time.
263	- A `DIAG-*` excalidraw artifact may carry `generated_by` pointing at the
264	  layer-1 run that produced it; regenerating updates the file and refreshes
265	  the source hash.
266	- Metric comparison and trend views are projections over runs, not evidence
267	  stored in a separate metric-owned runtime backend.
268	- A project may model acceptance criteria or test cases as standalone
269	  artifacts and link them to runs that emit pass/fail or richer structured
270	  results.
271	- A run may evaluate multiple governing artifacts together as long as those
272	  links are explicit in the run record.
273	
274	The shared rule is simple: artifact identity establishes meaning, authority,
275	and provenance; DDx runs establish repeatable runtime behavior and evidence.
276	
277	## Migration
278	
279	The dedicated `ddx adr` and `ddx sd` commands are removed. Their functionality is subsumed by:
280	
281	| Old Command | Replacement |
282	|-------------|-------------|
283	| `ddx adr create` | Agent creates file from template |
284	| `ddx adr list` | `ddx doc list --type ADR` |
285	| `ddx adr show ADR-001` | `ddx doc show ADR-001` |
286	| `ddx adr validate` | `ddx doc validate` |
287	| `ddx sd create` | Agent creates file from template |
288	| `ddx sd list` | `ddx doc list --type SD` |
289	| `ddx sd show SD-001` | `ddx doc show SD-001` |
290	| `ddx sd validate` | `ddx doc validate` |
291	
292	Existing markdown artifacts are unaffected by the multi-media broadening:
293	their embedded `ddx:` identity continues to work and `media_type` defaults
294	to `text/markdown` when absent. Adoption of sidecar identity is opt-in,
295	file by file.
296	
297	## Dependencies
298	
299	- FEAT-007 (Doc Graph) — provides the `ddx doc` commands, sidecar scanner,
300	  `media_type` handling, and `generated_by` provenance edges
301	- FEAT-010 (Runs) — provides the run records that `generated_by` references
302	- Document library with templates
303	
304	## Out of Scope
305	
306	- Type-specific CLI commands (use `ddx doc` generically)
307	- Content structure validation (that's dun/workflow-level)
308	- Template application commands (agents do this)
309	- A "managed asset" or any other second-class artifact tier
310

1	---
2	ddx:
3	  id: TD-005
4	  depends_on:
5	    - FEAT-005
6	    - FEAT-010
7	    - TD-010
8	    - SD-005
9	---
10	# Technical Design: Metric Runtime and History
11	
12	This design extends [TD-010](TD-010-executions.md).
13	Generic execution collection naming, attachment publication, and legacy
14	fallback rules come from the generic execution substrate; this document adds
15	the metric-specific definition fields, run payload shape, and comparison/trend
16	projection rules.
17	
18	## File Layout
19	
20	For the JSONL backend, the logical execution collections map to separate files:
21	
22	```text
23	.ddx/
24	├── exec-definitions.jsonl        # bead-backed execution definition records
25	├── exec-runs.jsonl               # bead-backed execution run records
26	└── exec-runs.d/
27	    └── <run-id>/
28	        ├── result.json
29	        ├── stdout.log
30	        └── stderr.log
31	```
32	
33	Metric-specific inspection is a projection over the generic execution store.
34	No separate `.ddx/metrics/` runtime store is required.
35	
36	Other backends preserve the same logical collections even if they do not map
37	them to JSONL files directly. Their physical storage layout is owned by the
38	backend implementation.
39	
40	## Execution Definition Format
41	
42	Each definition record is a bead-schema row in the `exec-definitions`
43	collection.
44	
45	```json
46	{
47	  "id": "exec-metric-startup-time@1",
48	  "issue_type": "exec_definition",
49	  "status": "open",
50	  "title": "Execution definition for MET-001 startup time",
51	  "labels": ["artifact:MET-001", "executor:command"],
52	  "artifact_ids": ["MET-001"],
53	  "executor": {
54	    "kind": "command",
55	    "command": ["ddx", "bead", "create"],
56	    "cwd": ".",
57	    "env": {
58	      "DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK": "1"
59	    }
60	  },
61	  "result": {
62	    "metric": {
63	      "unit": "ms",
64	      "value_path": "$.duration_ms"
65	    }
66	  },
67	  "evaluation": {
68	    "comparison": "lower-is-better",
69	    "thresholds": {
70	      "warn": 20,
71	      "ratchet": 30
72	    }
73	  },
74	  "active": true,
75	  "created_at": "2026-04-04T15:00:00Z"
76	}
77	```
78	
79	Rules:
80	- The execution definition is metadata, not executable code.
81	- The linked `artifact_ids` set must include exactly one `MET-*` artifact.
82	- `status=open` means the definition is active; `status=closed` means the
83	  definition is retired but preserved.
84	- Only one active definition should exist per metric at a time.
85	- Historical definitions remain readable for comparison and auditing.
86	
87	## Execution Run Format
88	
89	Each execution creates one execution-run bead row in the `exec-runs`
90	collection plus optional sidecar files under `exec-runs.d/<run-id>/`.
91	
92	```json
93	{
94	  "id": "exec-metric-startup-time@2026-04-04T15:01:00Z",
95	  "issue_type": "exec_run",
96	  "status": "closed",
97	  "title": "Execution run for MET-001 startup time",
98	  "labels": ["artifact:MET-001", "executor:command", "kind:metric"],
99	  "run_status": "success",
100	  "definition_id": "exec-metric-startup-time@1",
101	  "artifact_ids": ["MET-001"],
102	  "started_at": "2026-04-04T15:01:00Z",
103	  "finished_at": "2026-04-04T15:01:00Z",
104	  "result": {
105	    "metric": {
106	      "artifact_id": "MET-001",
107	      "definition_id": "exec-metric-startup-time@1",
108	      "observed_at": "2026-04-04T15:01:00Z",
109	      "status": "pass",
110	      "value": 14.6,
111	      "unit": "ms",
112	      "samples": [14.6],
113	      "comparison": {
114	        "baseline": 20,
115	        "delta": -5.4,
116	        "direction": "lower-is-better"
117	      }
118	    }
119	  },
120	  "exit_code": 0,
121	  "attachments": {
122	    "stdout": "exec-runs.d/exec-metric-startup-time@2026-04-04T15:01:00Z/stdout.log",
123	    "stderr": "exec-runs.d/exec-metric-startup-time@2026-04-04T15:01:00Z/stderr.log",
124	    "result": "exec-runs.d/exec-metric-startup-time@2026-04-04T15:01:00Z/result.json"
125	  },
126	  "provenance": {
127	    "git_rev": "abc123",
128	    "ddx_version": "0.1.0"
129	  }
130	}
131	```
132	
133	The execution-run row is the searchable metadata/index record. `result.json`
134	stores the structured metric payload referenced by the row. `stdout.log` and
135	`stderr.log` store raw captured output bodies when present.
136	
137	Migration policy:
138	- New writes target the bead-backed `exec-definitions` and `exec-runs`
139	  collections.
140	- Legacy `.ddx/exec/definitions/` and `.ddx/exec/runs/` data remains readable
141	  as a fallback during migration.
142	- `ddx metric` projections resolve through `ddx exec`; they do not write to a
143	  separate authoritative `.ddx/metrics/` runtime store.
144	
145	The bead-envelope `status` tracks record lifecycle. Domain execution outcome is
146	stored separately as `run_status` to avoid redefining the shared bead field.
147	
148	## Write Mechanism
149	
150	Execution definition and run writes must be safe under concurrent agents.
151	
152	1. Resolve the named bead-backed collection (`exec-definitions` or `exec-runs`).
153	2. Create any large payloads in a temporary attachment directory.
154	3. Acquire the collection lock.
155	4. Publish the attachment directory into its final location.
156	5. Create or update the bead-schema row in the collection using the existing
157	   bead store write path.
158	6. Release the lock.
159	
160	This reuses the bead store semantics for metadata writes while keeping large
161	payloads out of the collection row. A crash may leave an orphan attachment
162	directory, but it must not publish a partial row.
163	
164	## Validation Algorithm
165	
166	`ddx exec validate <definition-id>` performs:
167	
168	1. Resolve the linked `MET-*` artifact.
169	2. Resolve the execution definition by ID.
170	3. Verify the execution-definition bead row is valid for the collection.
171	4. Verify the executor configuration is present and executable.
172	5. Validate metric-specific result and evaluation fields.
173	6. Report actionable errors with the artifact ID, definition ID, and backing
174	   collection that failed.
175	
176	## Run Algorithm
177	
178	`ddx exec run <definition-id>` performs:
179	
180	1. Resolve the execution definition and linked `MET-*` artifact.
181	2. Spawn the command in the configured working directory.
182	3. Capture exit code, stdout, stderr, and elapsed duration.
183	4. Normalize the measured value(s) into `result.metric`.
184	5. Compute comparison output against the configured baseline.
185	6. Write the generic execution-run row and referenced attachments atomically
186	   enough that published rows never point at missing files.
187	
188	## Ratchet Evaluation and execute-bead Landing
189	
190	Metric ratchets feed into `ddx try` merge/preserve decisions.
191	The evaluation model is narrow: results → metrics → ratchet/blocker outcome.
192	
193	**Outcome rules:**
194	- A required execution that fails (non-zero exit or `run_status != "success"`)
195	  blocks the merge landing.
196	- A ratchet regression — where the current run's value exceeds the `ratchet`
197	  threshold in the direction that matters (`lower-is-better` or `higher-is-better`)
198	  relative to the configured threshold — prevents merge. The iteration is
199	  preserved under a hidden ref instead.
200	- Non-required, non-ratcheted metric runs are recorded evidence only; they do
201	  not affect the landing decision.
202	
203	**Threshold fields:**
204	- `thresholds.warn` — emit a warning when this value is exceeded; no blocking
205	- `thresholds.ratchet` — block landing when the current value strictly exceeds
206	  this threshold (`value > ratchet`, not equal-to). For multi-sample runs
207	  (multiple entries in `samples`), use the maximum value for threshold comparison
208	  unless the definition specifies otherwise.
209	
210	Field names are generic scalars, not unit-specific. The `unit` field in the
211	`result.metric` block identifies what the number means.
212	
213	### Acceptance Criteria (ratchet evaluation feeding execute-bead)
214	
215	- Given a metric execution run completes with a value exceeding `thresholds.ratchet` in the blocking direction, when execute-bead evaluates the outcome, then the iteration is classified as not merge-eligible and is preserved under a hidden ref rather than landed.
216	- Given a metric execution run completes with a value between `thresholds.warn` and `thresholds.ratchet`, when execute-bead evaluates the outcome, then a warning is emitted but the iteration remains merge-eligible.
217	- Given a non-required, non-ratcheted metric execution run completes, when execute-bead evaluates the outcome, then the run is stored as evidence and does not affect the merge/preserve decision.
218	
219	## Compare and Trend
220	
221	- Metric compare reads generic execution history, filters by `MET-*` artifact
222	  or definition ID, resolves the requested baseline, and reports a
223	  deterministic delta.
224	- Metric history filters execution-run rows by metric-linked artifact and
225	  prints them in observed order.
226	- Metric trend aggregates the `result.metric` series without rewriting any
227	  stored record.
228

/tmp/story-13-final.md
# Story 13 — Metric Artifact Type — Final Plan (post-codex)

## Diagnosis (unchanged from /tmp/story-13-plan.md)

The artifact-type vision for MET-* is half-built:
- FEAT-005 lists MET as a conventional type; docgraph is type-agnostic.
- `ddx metric validate/run/compare/history/trend` already key off MET-* IDs.
- `ddx.execution.metric.metric_id` already binds gates to metrics.
- TD-005 already says metric history persists through `exec-runs`.
- What's missing: no checked-in MET-* artifacts, no metric-specific frontmatter
  schema, no library template bundle, `ddx doc validate` does not enforce
  metric_id resolution, no `ddx metric list` / `show`, no versioning policy.

## Metric artifact schema (revised after codex)

```yaml
---
ddx:
  id: MET-007
  depends_on: [FEAT-014, SD-016]
metric:
  schema_version: 1                # ADDED: codex #4 — schema compatibility
  unit: ms                         # required, free-form
  direction: lower-is-better       # MUST match ComparisonLowerIsBetter / ComparisonHigherIsBetter
  goal: 250                        # RENAMED from `target` — aspirational/documentary
  budget: 400                      # ADDED — machine-checkable hard line; gate ratchet authoritative when both set
  source: exec                     # v1 enum: exec | external (codex #7 — derived dropped from v1)
  scope: per-attempt               # per-attempt | per-bead | per-feature | global
---
```

Notes:
- `direction` enum strings exactly match Go constants in
  `cli/internal/metric/types.go` (`lower-is-better`, `higher-is-better`).
- `goal` vs `budget` distinction (codex #2): goal = aspirational, budget =
  hard line. Gate `thresholds.ratchet` remains authoritative for landing
  decisions. `ddx doc validate` emits a **warning** (not error) when MET
  budget conflicts with the gate ratchet that observes it (codex #3).
- `source: derived` is **out of v1** (codex #7). Reintroduce only when a
  real derivation schema exists.
- Multi-node semantics (codex #5): MET-* is project-local. History rows
  in `exec-runs` may be partitioned per-node by routing metadata; `ddx
  metric show` reports partitions when present, never silently aggregates.
- Mixed-unit history (codex #4): `compare` and `trend` refuse to mix
  units; `ddx metric history` shows all rows but groups by unit; old rows
  remain valid evidence.

## Run-step linkage (unchanged)

1. Gate → metric: `ddx.execution.metric.metric_id: MET-NNN` (exists).
2. MET → contributing gates: reverse index in `ddx metric show`.
3. AC → MET citation: prose-only in v1 (codex #6 — defer structured
   `acceptance:` block until DDx validates bead ACs).

No new bead-event kinds. No new storage collection (codex #11; TD-005
confirms `exec-runs` already houses metric history).

## Spec changes

- **FEAT-005:** add §"Type-specific metadata example: MET" with the
  schema above (parallel to REF example).
- **TD-005:** brief amendment noting (a) `schema_version`, (b) mixed-unit
  refusal in compare/trend, (c) reverse-index for `metric show`.
- **FEAT-016:** cross-reference — process metrics rows decorated when
  ID matches an MET-* artifact.
- **FEAT-014:** brief note that runtime metrics MAY be promoted to MET-*.
- **No FEAT-020/021 changes** in v1 (multi-node aggregation deferred;
  per-MET dashboards are a follow-on).

## CLI surface (delta)

- `ddx metric list` — **add**; thin wrapper over `ddx doc list --type
  MET` joined with `metric.Store.History` for last-observed value/status.
- `ddx metric show <MET-id>` — **add**; frontmatter + reverse-index of
  observing gates + history partition by unit.
- `ddx doc validate` — **add rule**: every `execution.metric.metric_id`
  must resolve to an existing MET-* artifact (error); gate.ratchet vs
  MET.budget mismatch (warning).
- **Fix existing bug** (codex #1): `cli/cmd/metric.go:32`
  `resolveMetricDefinition` picks `defs[0]`; replace with
  `metric.Store.LoadDefinition` which already selects latest active
  deterministically.
- **Help text** (codex #1): clarify `ddx metric run` is a MET-ID
  convenience wrapper around `ddx exec run`.
- **README cleanup** (codex #14): the README claims
  `ddx metric define/log/result` exist — they do not. Trim the README
  to actual verbs (validate/run/compare/history/trend/list/show) as
  part of B3.

## Acceptance criteria

1. `library/artifacts/met/{template,create,evolve,check}.md` exists.
   Template renders the v1 schema (with `schema_version: 1`).
2. FEAT-005 documents the `metric:` block; TD-005 amended for
   schema_version + mixed-unit + reverse-index.
3. `ddx doc validate` errors when any
   `execution.metric.metric_id` does not resolve; warns when
   gate.ratchet conflicts with MET.budget. Test fixtures cover both
   cases.
4. `ddx metric list` returns MET-* docs with id, title, unit,
   direction, goal, budget, latest_value, latest_status; supports
   `--json`; empty-repo returns empty list (not error).
5. `ddx metric show MET-NNN` shows frontmatter + prose + observing gates
   + history partitioned by unit. `--json` shape documented.
6. ≥2 real MET-* artifacts checked into `docs/metrics/`:
   - MET-001 (e.g. ddx-test-walltime, source=exec, goal+budget set)
   - MET-002 (e.g. cost-per-closed-bead, source=external, goal only)
   Each reachable from a root.
7. Integration test (codex #13): fixture project with
   `execution.metric.metric_id: MET-002` → run → `exec-runs` row
   appears → `ddx metric show MET-002` lists the gate and the
   observation → `ddx metric history MET-002` shows the value with
   correct unit.
8. `cli/cmd/metric.go:32` no longer uses `defs[0]`; uses
   `metric.Store.LoadDefinition`. Test pins deterministic selection.
9. README CLI command list trimmed to actually-implemented verbs.
10. `ddx metric compare` / `trend` refuse to operate on mixed-unit
    history (returns explicit error); test covers.

## Tests

- `cli/internal/docgraph/validate_test.go` — unresolved metric_id;
  valid metric_id; gate.ratchet vs MET.budget mismatch warning.
- `cli/internal/metric/store_test.go` — reverse-index resolution;
  mixed-unit refusal in Compare/Trend; partition-by-unit in History.
- `cli/cmd/metric_acceptance_test.go` — `ddx metric list`,
  `ddx metric show` golden output (text + --json), deterministic
  definition selection.
- New fixture: `cli/cmd/testdata/met-artifacts/` with two MET docs +
  gate doc referencing each.
- Integration round-trip test under `cli/cmd/` exercising
  gate→run→exec-runs→show pipeline.
- No new e2e for v1; web UI deferred.

## Risks

- Schema lock-in too early — mitigated by `schema_version: 1`.
- Two sources of truth (MET.budget vs gate.ratchet) — gate authoritative;
  validate emits warning on conflict; revisit if drift becomes painful.
- Reverse-index cost — defer cache until measured.
- Deferred derived/multi-node/AC-comparator work could fragment if
  follow-on stories don't pick them up. Track in a "MET v2" backlog note
  on FEAT-005 (one paragraph, not a separate bead).

## Bead breakdown (revised — split B1 per codex #12)

- **B1a — FEAT-005 amendment + library/artifacts/met bundle**
  - Amend FEAT-005 with §"Type-specific metadata example: MET".
  - Amend TD-005 with schema_version + mixed-unit + reverse-index notes.
  - Create `library/artifacts/met/{template,create,evolve,check}.md`.
  - AC: docs render; templates install via `ddx init`; existing tests pass.
  - Tier: cheap.

- **B1b — Author first MET-* artifacts** (deps: B1a)
  - Author MET-001 (exec-source, e.g. ddx test wall time) with goal
    + budget; wire to an existing gate.
  - Author MET-002 (external-source, e.g. cost-per-closed-bead) with
    goal only.
  - Both reachable from a root.
  - AC: `ddx doc validate` clean; `ddx doc list --type MET` returns 2.
  - Tier: cheap (deciding real values is judgment-light here; pick
    plausible starting numbers).

- **B2 — `ddx doc validate` enforces metric_id; warns on
  budget/ratchet conflict** (deps: B1a)
  - Add rule + warning + test fixtures.
  - AC: dangling metric_id errors; valid passes; ratchet/budget
    conflict warns; `--json` surfaces error/warning codes.
  - Tier: cheap.

- **B3 — `ddx metric list` + `show` + bug fix + README cleanup**
  (deps: B1a, B1b)
  - Implement list and show; reverse-index over docgraph; partition by
    unit; deterministic-definition fix at metric.go:32.
  - Trim README CLI command list.
  - Update help text on `ddx metric run` to clarify wrapper semantics.
  - AC: golden tests pass; `--json` shapes documented; empty-repo safe;
    metric.go:32 uses LoadDefinition; README accurate.
  - Tier: standard.

- **B4 — Integration round-trip + mixed-unit refusal**
  (deps: B1b, B3)
  - Add integration test gate→run→exec-runs→show.
  - Add Compare/Trend mixed-unit refusal + History partition tests.
  - AC: tests pass; failure modes have clear error messages.
  - Tier: standard.

- **B5 — FEAT-014 / FEAT-016 cross-reference amendments**
  (deps: B1a)
  - Brief edits noting MET-* promotion path.
  - AC: spec docs updated; no code change.
  - Tier: cheap.

(Web UI per-MET page, derived metrics with a real formula schema,
multi-node aggregation, structured AC comparator, and any default
MET-ESCALATION-* metric remain explicitly out of Story 13. Track in
a "MET v2" backlog note on FEAT-005 per codex #10.)

## Coordination
(Bash completed with no output)

2 integrity issue(s) found

input.old_string:
Identity (`id`, `depends_on`, etc.) stays under `ddx:`; type-specific fields
like `source_url`, `source_author`, and `accessed` live at the top level.

#### `media_type`
input.new_string:
Identity (`id`, `depends_on`, etc.) stays under `ddx:`; type-specific fields
like `source_url`, `source_author`, and `accessed` live at the top level.

#### Type-specific metadata example: MET

Metric artifacts (`MET-*`) carry a top-level `metric:` block alongside the
`ddx:` identity block. The block is type-specific metadata that the generic
parser passes through inert; metric tooling (`ddx metric`, `ddx doc validate`)
keys off it.

```yaml
---
ddx:
  id: MET-007
  depends_on: [FEAT-014, SD-016]
metric:
  schema_version: 1            # required; v1 is the only accepted value
  unit: ms                     # required; free-form (ms, bytes, USD, count, ...)
  direction: lower-is-better   # required; lower-is-better | higher-is-better
  goal: 250                    # optional; aspirational target (documentary)
  budget: 400                  # optional; machine-checkable hard line
  source: exec                 # required; v1 enum: exec | external
  scope: per-attempt           # required; per-attempt | per-bead | per-feature | global
---
# Metric: ...
```

Field semantics:

- `schema_version` — pins the metric block schema. v1 is the only accepted
  value; future versions extend rather than replace.
- `unit` — free-form unit string. Mixed-unit history is refused by
  comparison and trend projections (see TD-005).
- `direction` — must match the comparison constants used by the metric
  runtime (`lower-is-better`, `higher-is-better`).
- `goal` vs `budget` — `goal` is aspirational and documentary; `budget`
  is a machine-checkable hard line. When a gate's `thresholds.ratchet`
  observes this metric, the gate ratchet is authoritative for landing
  decisions; `ddx doc validate` emits a warning when `budget` and the
  observing ratchet disagree.
- `source` — `exec` for metrics emitted by `ddx exec` runs; `external`
  for metrics ingested from outside DDx (e.g. cost reports).
- `scope` — declares the granularity at which the value is meaningful.

`source: derived` is intentionally out of v1; it returns when a real
derivation schema exists. `MET-*` artifacts are project-local; multi-node
aggregation is deferred to a future MET v2.

> **MET v2 backlog (informational):** derived-source metrics with a real
> formula schema, multi-node aggregation semantics, structured acceptance-
> criteria comparators, and per-MET dashboard wiring are deferred. None of
> these are required by v1; they are tracked here so future work can pick
> them up without rediscovering the shape.

#### `media_type`

input.old_string:
## Compare and Trend

- Metric compare reads generic execution history, filters by `MET-*` artifact
  or definition ID, resolves the requested baseline, and reports a
  deterministic delta.
- Metric history filters execution-run rows by metric-linked artifact and
  prints them in observed order.
- Metric trend aggregates the `result.metric` series without rewriting any
  stored record.
input.new_string:
## Compare and Trend

- Metric compare reads generic execution history, filters by `MET-*` artifact
  or definition ID, resolves the requested baseline, and reports a
  deterministic delta.
- Metric history filters execution-run rows by metric-linked artifact and
  prints them in observed order.
- Metric trend aggregates the `result.metric` series without rewriting any
  stored record.

## Collection Ownership and v1 Amendments

Metric history is owned by the generic `exec-runs` collection defined in
TD-010. There is no separate `metric-runs` collection and no
`.ddx/metrics/` runtime store. `ddx metric` is a projection over
`exec-runs` filtered by `MET-*` artifact id; metric reads and writes flow
through the same execution substrate as every other `ddx exec` consumer.

The following v1 amendments apply to the metric projection without
changing the collection layout:

- **`schema_version`** — the `metric:` frontmatter block on a `MET-*`
  artifact (see FEAT-005) carries `schema_version: 1`. Metric tooling
  refuses to load a `MET-*` artifact whose `schema_version` is absent or
  not equal to the value(s) it understands. Rows already persisted in
  `exec-runs` remain readable regardless of the artifact's current
  `schema_version`; the version gates artifact interpretation, not
  history reads.
- **Mixed-unit refusal in compare and trend** — `ddx metric compare` and
  `ddx metric trend` refuse to operate when the selected
  `result.metric` rows span more than one `unit`, returning an explicit
  error. `ddx metric history` continues to surface every row but groups
  output by `unit` so older rows under a retired unit remain visible
  evidence rather than silently mixed into a current series.
- **Reverse-index for `metric show`** — `ddx metric show <MET-id>`
  resolves the set of execution definitions whose `result.metric`
  observes the named `MET-*` artifact (the gates contributing
  observations) by walking the docgraph plus `exec-definitions`. This is
  a read-time projection; no new index is persisted.

These amendments preserve the bead-backed `exec-definitions` /
`exec-runs` collection ownership documented above. No rename to
`metric-runs` is implied or planned.

input.content:
---
ddx:
  id: MET-NNN
  depends_on: []
metric:
  schema_version: 1
  unit: <unit>                  # ms | bytes | USD | count | ratio | ...
  direction: lower-is-better    # lower-is-better | higher-is-better
  goal: <number>                # optional aspirational target
  budget: <number>              # optional machine-checkable hard line
  source: exec                  # exec | external
  scope: per-attempt            # per-attempt | per-bead | per-feature | global
---
# Metric: <Short Name>

## What this measures

One paragraph describing the observable, the system boundary, and what a
reader is meant to infer from a single value. Be specific about what is
included and what is excluded.

## Why it matters

Tie the metric back to the governing artifact(s) listed in `ddx.depends_on`.
Explain the decision the metric supports — for example, gating a release,
catching a regression, or guiding a tradeoff.

## How it is observed

Describe the producer of the value:

- **`source: exec`** — name the `ddx exec` definition that emits this
  metric and the field path it populates (e.g. `result.metric.value`).
- **`source: external`** — name the upstream system or report, the
  ingestion pathway, and the cadence.

## Goal vs budget

- **Goal (`<number> <unit>`)** — aspirational target. Documentary only.
- **Budget (`<number> <unit>`)** — hard line. When a gate observes this
  metric via `ddx.execution.metric.metric_id`, the gate's
  `thresholds.ratchet` is authoritative for landing decisions. `ddx doc
  validate` warns when budget and the observing ratchet disagree.

## History and projection

Metric history lives in the generic `exec-runs` collection (see TD-005).
Read it via:

- `ddx metric history MET-NNN` — observed values in order
- `ddx metric show MET-NNN`    — frontmatter, observing gates, partitions
- `ddx metric compare MET-NNN <baseline>` — deterministic delta (refuses
  to mix units)
- `ddx metric trend MET-NNN`   — aggregated series (refuses to mix units)

## Notes

Optional. Capture caveats: known noise sources, sampling assumptions,
unit migrations, or links to follow-on work in the MET v2 backlog.

input.content:
# Prompt: author a MET-* metric artifact

You are creating a new metric artifact (`MET-NNN`) under `docs/metrics/`
or another configured artifact root. The artifact must comply with the
schema defined in FEAT-005 §"Type-specific metadata example: MET" and the
runtime contract in TD-005.

## Inputs

- `<short-name>` — kebab-case slug for the metric (e.g. `ddx-test-walltime`)
- `<governing-ids>` — IDs of the artifacts this metric serves (PRD,
  feature spec, technical design)
- `<observable>` — one-sentence description of what is being measured
- `<source>` — `exec` (emitted by a `ddx exec` definition) or `external`

## Steps

1. Allocate a fresh `MET-NNN` ID (use `ddx doc next-id MET` if available;
   otherwise pick the next free integer).
2. Copy `library/artifacts/met/template.md` to the destination (typically
   `docs/metrics/MET-NNN-<short-name>.md`).
3. Fill the frontmatter:
   - `ddx.id` → the allocated ID
   - `ddx.depends_on` → `<governing-ids>`
   - `metric.schema_version` → always `1` for v1
   - `metric.unit` → the actual unit string (no synonyms; pick one and
     stick with it — mixed units are refused by compare and trend)
   - `metric.direction` → `lower-is-better` or `higher-is-better`; must
     match the constants the runtime uses
   - `metric.goal` and `metric.budget` — set whichever you can defend.
     Prefer omitting a field over guessing.
   - `metric.source` → matches `<source>`
   - `metric.scope` → narrowest level at which the value is meaningful
4. Write the prose sections:
   - **What this measures** — define the observable and its boundary
   - **Why it matters** — link the decision back to `depends_on`
   - **How it is observed** — name the producer (`ddx exec` definition
     for `source: exec`; upstream system + cadence for `source: external`)
   - **Goal vs budget** — restate the values and what enforcement they
     get; note that gate `thresholds.ratchet` is authoritative when both
     the gate and `metric.budget` are set
5. If `source: exec`, ensure (or file a follow-up bead to ensure) a
   matching `ddx exec` definition exists with
   `ddx.execution.metric.metric_id: MET-NNN`. The producer must be in
   place before the metric can collect history.
6. Run `ddx doc audit` (or `ddx doc validate`) and fix any new errors
   the new artifact introduces. The metric must be reachable from a
   root via `depends_on`.

## Quality bar

- The frontmatter parses, all required `metric:` fields are present,
  and `schema_version` is exactly `1`.
- `unit` and `direction` are concrete, not placeholders.
- The artifact is reachable from a root in the doc graph.
- If `source: exec`, the metric ID is referenced by at least one
  `ddx exec` definition (or a follow-up bead exists to wire one).
- Prose is specific: a future maintainer can tell what is included
  in the measurement and what is excluded.

## Anti-patterns

- Inventing a third `source` value beyond `exec | external`. `derived`
  is reserved for v2 and must not appear in v1 artifacts.
- Picking a `direction` string that does not match the runtime
  constants (`lower-is-better`, `higher-is-better`).
- Setting `metric.budget` without coordinating with the observing gate's
  `thresholds.ratchet`. They will disagree and `ddx doc validate` will
  warn.
- Mixing units across history rows by editing `unit` after observations
  exist. Retire the metric and create a new one instead.

input.content:
# MET-* examples

Two reference shapes plugin authors can copy when authoring metric
artifacts. Both conform to the v1 schema in FEAT-005 and the runtime
contract in TD-005.

## Example 1: `source: exec`, goal + budget

A metric emitted by a `ddx exec` definition. The metric has both an
aspirational goal and a machine-checkable budget. The observing gate's
`thresholds.ratchet` remains authoritative for landing decisions.

```markdown
---
ddx:
  id: MET-001
  depends_on: [FEAT-001, SD-001]
metric:
  schema_version: 1
  unit: ms
  direction: lower-is-better
  goal: 250
  budget: 400
  source: exec
  scope: per-attempt
---
# Metric: ddx test wall time

## What this measures

Wall-clock duration of `cd cli && go test ./...` on the reference
runner, measured by the `exec-ddx-test-walltime` execution definition.
Excludes build cache warm-up and post-test artifact upload.

## Why it matters

Test wall time is the inner-loop cost of every contributor change. A
budget violation reliably degrades the FEAT-001 contributor experience.

## How it is observed

`ddx exec` definition `exec-ddx-test-walltime@1` runs the suite and
emits `result.metric.value` in milliseconds. History accumulates in the
`exec-runs` collection.

## Goal vs budget

- Goal: 250 ms — aspirational
- Budget: 400 ms — gate `thresholds.ratchet` on the observing
  definition is authoritative; `ddx doc validate` warns if the two
  drift apart.
```

## Example 2: `source: external`, goal only

A metric ingested from an upstream system. No budget is set because the
value is informational rather than gating. `goal` documents the target.

```markdown
---
ddx:
  id: MET-002
  depends_on: [helix.prd]
metric:
  schema_version: 1
  unit: USD
  direction: lower-is-better
  goal: 0.50
  source: external
  scope: per-bead
---
# Metric: cost per closed bead

## What this measures

Average inference + tooling cost attributable to closing one bead,
sourced from the weekly cost report exported into `exec-runs` via the
`ingest-cost-report` external pipeline.

## Why it matters

Tracks the unit economics of agent-driven development against the PRD
sustainability target.

## How it is observed

External ingestion: the weekly cost report writes one row per closed
bead into the `exec-runs` collection with
`labels: ["artifact:MET-002"]` and `result.metric.value` in USD.

## Goal vs budget

- Goal: 0.50 USD — aspirational; trend matters more than any single
  observation. No budget is set; this metric does not gate landing.
```

## What to copy

- The frontmatter shape is the contract. The prose section names are
  recommendations from `template.md`; reorder if a project convention
  prefers something else, but keep the section meanings intact.
- For `source: exec`, you must wire a `ddx exec` definition that
  references the `MET-*` ID (or file a follow-up bead to do so).
- For `source: external`, document the ingestion pathway and cadence
  in the prose so a reader can verify the rows in `exec-runs`.

data.text:
Committed `dc565a65`. All four AC met:
1. FEAT-005 has the v1 `metric:` block schema in a new "Type-specific metadata example: MET" subsection.
2. TD-005 has a new "Collection Ownership and v1 Amendments" section confirming `exec-runs` ownership (no rename to `metric-runs`), plus schema_version, mixed-unit refusal, and reverse-index notes.
3. `library/artifacts/met/{template,prompt,examples}.md` bundle created.
4. `ddx doc audit` is clean relative to baseline (the 2 pre-existing cycles in ADR-007/FEAT-026 and FEAT-002 chain are unchanged).
